text:
2. Add an NMP-owned X-Ray projection or nmp-devtools stream. It should expose ordered open/replace/refresh/close events, scope/projection owner, reason/provenance, transaction/
     revision, shared-owner/refcount state, and teardown cascade. [...]   3. Join Trellis reconciliation events to NMP kernel outcomes. NMP already applies Trellis ResourcePlan as ApplyDependentInterestDelta; now it needs to retain/export bounded diagnostic
     records instead of test-only traces. [...]   4. Reuse existing relay diagnostics for the relay/socket side: wire subscriptions, relay rows, consumer counts, and event counts already exist. X-Ray needs to correlate those with the
     reconciliation command stream. [...] - **Phase A — ADR-0075 amendment** (#2809): define "diagnostic surface" vs "app surface," and update doctrine-lint plus the Trellis public-API leak ratchet (#2642) so the exemption is enforced mechanically, not case-by-case.
- **Phase B — nmp-devtools stream**: ordered open/replace/refresh/close events with owner/provenance/revision/refcount/teardown data; join Trellis `ResourcePlan` → `ApplyDependentInterestDelta` outcomes into bounded retained records (today they're test-only traces); correlate with the existing relay diagnostics; and define the **NMP-owned receipt shape** so Chirp never sees `ResourcePlan`/`ResourceKey`/`Graph`. [...] **7. [Should-fix — Phase B] The receipt shape is missing fields its own cause-chain examples require.** The shape bullet (projection key, view/scope label, interest shape, owner key, owner count, relay rows, reason string) omits: transaction id + revision + sequence number (the first Phase B bullet promises them — unify); wall-clock timestamp (`TransactionTrace` has none; NMP must stamp it); command kind (open/replace/refresh/close); outcome/status (a CLOSE that failed at the socket looks identical to one that succeeded); owner-count **transition** (the epic's own example is "refcount 2→1", but the shape only has a current count); parent-scope reference (teardown cascades ordered "children first" need hierarchy); and a structured cause/parent link for `why_subscription_open` to return. Also flag "interest shape" explicitly as the primary privacy-bearing field — it's a Nostr filter containing pubkeys/event ids, and Trellis redaction never sees it. [...] **Other findings that changed the epic:** capsules need a **symbolication manifest** (traces are keyed by opaque NodeIds — unreadable to anyone who didn't see the session without an embedded label registry); Trellis trace versioning is exact-match-only, so the capsule needs its own **envelope version + compat policy** or every capsule attached to an old issue dies on the first format bump; the Phase D **transport decision moved into Phase A** because the lint-exemption wording depends on it; free-form reason strings became **structured reason codes** (type lints can't catch a Debug-formatted `ResourcePlan` leaking into a string); the receipt shape gained the fields its own examples required (owner-count *transition*, outcome status, parent scope, timestamps); my "agent answers why-is-my-feed-empty" exit criterion was rightly called untestable and is now a **seeded failure-scenario corpus** against a local relay fixture — deliberately including the "subscription fine, zero events came back" case, which receipts alone can't answer; and the **headless NMP host runner** is now itemized as its own (probably largest) Phase C work item. [...] - **PRs #2860/#2861** — `XrayReceipt` + bounded ordered `XrayReceiptStream` + the Trellis→receipt adapter. Most of Phase B.
- **PR #2862** — `XrayCapsule` envelope (schema versions, producer metadata, redaction mode, **symbolication manifest**), stable shareable pseudonymization, and `XrayProbe` helpers for all five debug queries. That's the Fable-review findings implemented — whoever built it worked straight from the updated epic.
 [...] I synced epic #2858 (checkboxes ticked with PR references, status comment posted). **What's actually left**, in order: wire the live feed-session reconciler into the receipt stream (Phase B's last gap — probes over an unfed stream prove nothing), then the MCP server + headless host runner + seeded acceptance corpus (Phase C), the Chirp sidecar client + pane (Phase D), and Phase E residuals (recorder + byte-bounded ring buffer + export, reader compat policy, and merging trellis#162 for the redaction leak). The updated coding-agent brief is: **"work the live reconciler→XrayReceiptStream join per epic #2858 Phase B"** — everything downstream now waits on that.
